nodes.name: tenants
nodes.summary: Root multi-tenant entity storing business identity, status, subscription tier, and soft-delete metadata. All other tenant-scoped tables cascade from this table via foreign key.

nodes.name: profiles
nodes.summary: Links Supabase auth users to their tenant, storing display name and phone number with format validation constraints.

nodes.name: clients
nodes.summary: Stores business clients per tenant with contact details, running balance, and status. Phone number is unique per tenant.

nodes.name: products
nodes.summary: Catalog of products or services offered by a tenant, with name, price, and optional description.

nodes.name: invoices
nodes.summary: Records invoices issued by a tenant to a client for a product, with status lifecycle (pending/paid/partial/overdue) and unique invoice numbers per tenant.

nodes.name: transactions
nodes.summary: Captures financial transactions (sales or payments) per tenant-client pair, optionally linked to an invoice.

nodes.name: notifications
nodes.summary: In-app notification records for tenant users, supporting invoice, payment, client, product, and system event types.

nodes.name: payment_details
nodes.summary: Stores per-tenant M-Pesa payment configuration entries (paybill, till, or account number) with active/inactive status.

nodes.name: user_roles
nodes.summary: Associates auth users with application roles (admin, user, client, superadmin) with uniqueness enforced per user-role pair.

nodes.name: super_admins
nodes.summary: Registry of platform-level super and support administrators, identified by user_id and email, with active status and last login tracking.

nodes.name: super_admin_audit_logs
nodes.summary: Immutable audit trail for super admin actions, capturing action type, affected resource, tenant, IP address, and user agent for governance.

nodes.name: tenant_subscriptions
nodes.summary: Tracks per-tenant subscription plan (free/pro/enterprise) with usage limits for users, clients, invoices, and products, plus billing lifecycle dates.

nodes.name: tenant_feature_flags
nodes.summary: Per-tenant feature flag toggles enabling runtime feature gating by flag name without code deploys.

nodes.name: tenant_usage_metrics
nodes.summary: Periodic usage snapshots per tenant recording user, client, invoice, and transaction counts alongside API calls and storage bytes for billing and analytics.

nodes.name: recreate_database.sql
nodes.summary: Complete Supabase database recreation script defining all 14 tables, 5 ENUM types, 1 view, 42 indexes, Row Level Security policies, and 15+ PL/pgSQL functions and triggers for the multi-tenant platform. Includes super admin governance, subscription management, and automated business logic triggers.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Supabase-specific patterns including auth.uid(), auth.jwt(), SECURITY DEFINER functions, and RLS policies. PL/pgSQL triggers maintain denormalized running balances and invoice statuses automatically.

nodes.name: tenants (trial column)
nodes.summary: Migration target: adds trial_ends_at column to tenants with a 7-day default and backfills existing rows.

nodes.name: tenant_subscriptions (paystack columns)
nodes.summary: Migration target: adds Paystack integration columns (customer code, subscription code, next payment date, cancellation timestamp) to tenant_subscriptions.

nodes.name: add_subscription_columns.sql
nodes.summary: Additive migration extending the subscription model with trial period tracking on tenants and Paystack payment gateway fields on tenant_subscriptions. Includes trigger and helper function for automated trial assignment.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION with SECURITY DEFINER and a BEFORE INSERT trigger to auto-assign trials on tenant creation. The get_tenant_subscription_status function returns a 4-state enum string: trial, active, cancelled, or expired.

nodes.name: config.toml
nodes.summary: Supabase project configuration specifying the project ID and disabling JWT verification for four Edge Functions: reset-password, resolve-login-email, create-client-user, and send-transaction-sms.
nodes.languageNotes: verify_jwt = false on these functions allows unauthenticated invocation, which is required for signup flows and SMS delivery that run before a session exists.
